# Real-world fixture

`facebook-kag-conversion-data-original.csv` is a genuine, publicly published Facebook ad account dataset (1,143 real ad records across 3 real campaigns, widely cited as the "KAG conversion data" set — [source](https://github.com/mGalarnyk/Python_Tutorials/blob/master/Kaggle/Facebook/KAG_conversion_data.csv)), used here to prove GrowthOps ingests genuine external ad-platform data, not just its own synthetic fixtures.

`facebook-kag-conversion-data-meta-format.csv` re-maps the same real numbers (aggregated per campaign: real spend, impressions, clicks, conversions) into our Meta Ads Manager export column format, so it can be uploaded directly through the app's CSV import. It imports successfully and produces correct computed metrics (CPC, CPM, CPA).

**Known limitation, stated honestly:** this dataset has no date dimension — it is a single-point-in-time snapshot, not a daily time series. GrowthOps' detectors (budget bleeder, scaling winner, CPA breach, spend pacing anomaly) all require a multi-day trailing window to judge a trend, so importing this file alone produces normalized campaign data but correctly generates **zero** recommended actions — there is no trend yet to judge. This is expected, verified behavior, not a bug: genuine per-day ad-platform exports with real dollar figures are not published publicly for privacy/commercial reasons, so no freely available real dataset has the shape needed to drive the detectors directly. The demo's day-over-day story (`growthops:demo-seed`) uses realistic synthetic data for that reason, and says so plainly in the main README.
